Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Explicitly stated in Results and Accession numbers as CsTT8-2 (Csa02g028180.1).

Source_Species.reason: The paper studies camelina/Camelina sativa genes.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Camelina sativa is the species named throughout the paper.

Applied_Species.reason: Gene editing and phenotyping were performed in camelina.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species is Camelina sativa in the experiments.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Expression and mutant analyses used camelina variety Suneson/WT Suneson.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Expression patterns were investigated in camelina variety Suneson.

Regulator_Type.reason: TT8 is described as a transcription factor gene; predicted proteins are TT8 transcription factors.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results state the proteins contain an N-terminal MYB interaction region (MIR) and a C-terminal bHLH domain.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No nucleus/nuclear localization experiment or explicit statement appears in the paper text.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results directly connect TT8 editing with seed TAG, total fatty acids, and flavonoid content.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These products are lipids/fatty acids and flavonoids, as directly discussed.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Measurements were made in seeds and the traits are seed-specific.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper explicitly frames oil as feedstock/biofuel value and flavonoids as responsible for seed coat pigmentation.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Supported for combined loss with the other two homeologs: triple mutants had yellow seeds, reduced flavonoids, reduced mucilage, and increased TFA/TAG.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is supported at the homeolog-family/triple-mutant level; CsTT8-2 is one of three seed-expressed TT8 genes and the numeric flavonoid/TFA changes are reported.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods appear in Results/Methods: CRISPR editing, Sanger genotyping, RT-qPCR, RNA-seq, flavonoid and mucilage quantification, GC/GC-MS lipid profiling, and 14C-acetate incorporation.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results show high developing-seed expression peaking around 10–12 DAF, lower in roots, barely detectable in leaves/stems.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The same seed-high, 10–12 DAF peak expression pattern is shown in camelina.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Growth chamber conditions and developing seed stages 10–24 DAF/12 and 24 DAF are described.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Disruption of CsTT8 increased LEC1/LEC2/FUS3 and downstream FA genes, supporting that TT8 acts as a negative transcriptional regulator/repressor in this context.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Results directly support roles in seed coat flavonoid accumulation, mucilage formation, and fatty acid biosynthesis/oil accumulation.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: LEC1, LEC2, FUS3, WRI1, BCCP1/2, KASI/II/III, FAD2, FAD3 expression changes are measured, but direct targeting was not tested here; CT is mentioned generically, and GL2/TTG2 are only discussed with Figure S7/supporting context.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Results show increased expression of LEC1, LEC2, FUS3, WRI1 and several FA synthesis genes, elevated FAD2 and small decrease in FAD3; GL2/TTG2 decrease is presented as 'may' and thus only tentative, but the overall field wording reflects that nuance.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: The paper shows TT8 disruption shifts metabolism toward higher seed oil and lower flavonoid accumulation.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Seed developmental expression is shown, but the paper does not explicitly define an upstream signal/input controlling CsTT8 as 'seed developmental program'.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: MBW complex involvement is inferred from prior knowledge/discussion, not directly demonstrated in the Results for camelina CsTT8 proteins.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: No interacting proteins or MYB/TTG1 partners were experimentally validated in this paper.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results/Discussion support redundancy of the three homeologs for seed color and that CsTT8-8 had the highest expression; phenotypes were shown for triple mutants, not single-gene knockouts alone.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Figure S6/Results report slight hundred-seed-weight decreases in two lines, no significant starch/protein change, and no obvious growth defects.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Summary/Discussion explicitly present TT8 editing as useful for creating higher-oil yellow-seeded camelina germplasm for biofuel/bioproduct production.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq data are explicitly referenced for expression profiling, and no accession is provided in the paper.

corrections.reason: No nucleus/nuclear localization experiment or explicit statement appears in the paper text.

corrections.old_value: MYB partners (MBW complex, not individually validated in this paper); TTG1-complex context
corrections.reason: No interacting proteins or MYB/TTG1 partners were experimentally validated in this paper.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results show CsTT8-8 is highly expressed in developing seeds, highest among homeologs, with much lower expression in roots and barely detectable in leaves/stems.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Editing increases lipogenic TF and FA-synthesis gene expression, indicating negative regulation by TT8 in this context.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Supported by results on flavonoids, mucilage, and FA/oil traits.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: Expression changes for many genes are reported, but direct target status was not demonstrated in this paper; GL2/TTG2 are especially speculative/supporting-information based.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: The measured transcriptional changes after CsTT8 editing are explicitly described, with caveat that GL2/TTG2 are only suggested.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: A developmental expression pattern is shown, but no explicit upstream input is established.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly says CsTT8-8 has the highest expression, yet all three genes are required for the yellow-seed phenotype, indicating redundancy.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Supported by slight seed-weight decrease in two lines, unchanged starch/protein, and no visible growth defects.
